title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Hinge is expanding its One More Hour social impact initiative with a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London that put on free or affordable events for young people. The effort is presented as a response to growing loneliness among Gen Z and aims to encourage in-person social activities.
bullets: Gen Z is increasingly struggling with loneliness.
Hinge announced a $1 million fund for social groups.
Target cities: New York, Los Angeles and London.
Funds will support free or affordable social events for young people.
This is a global expansion of Hinge's One More Hour initiative.
takeaways: Monitor localized traffic patterns in NY, LA and London during event promotions and registration periods for potential short-term demand spikes.
Coordinate capacity planning with platform clients when they run local, event-driven campaigns to anticipate transient load.
Prioritize reliable, low-latency connectivity in urban points of presence where in-person events are being promoted.
Track major apps' investments in offline events as signals of concentrated, time-bound increases in digital engagement tied to real-world activities.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session demonstrates how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent understands your business. The demo is end-to-end and requires no prior graph expertise.
bullets: Knowledge graphs address the gap where general AI agents lack company-specific knowledge.
The session builds a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents.
The graph is connected to an AI agent to enable business-aware responses.
No graph query or graph theory expertise is required for the workflow.
An end-to-end demo is shown; a related episode compares VectorDB vs GraphDB.
takeaways: Equip AI agents with your operational and documentation context to make them useful for enterprise-specific tasks.
Automated graph construction reduces the need to hire specialist graph engineers, lowering deployment barriers.
End-to-end pipelines can accelerate putting domain-aware AI into production for faster value realization.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, when leaders think about tax credits for sustainability, energy incentives often come to mind first but they’re only part of the picture. Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner at PwC US, highlights less obvious supports—state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation—arguing that a broader view of sustainability investments can unlock meaningful tax value.
bullets: Energy incentives are common but not the only tax levers for sustainability.
State and local abatements can support project economics.
Location-based incentives matter for siting and expansion decisions.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation in processes and packaging.
Viewing sustainability investments broadly can reveal additional tax value.
takeaways: Factor state and local abatements into site selection and project ROI analyses.
Assess location-based incentives when planning new builds, expansions, or relocations.
Identify R&D-eligible work tied to process or operational innovation to capture credits.
Incorporate a broad tax-incentive review (beyond energy credits) into sustainability project planning to maximize value.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the video shows Copilot helping Christa find her inner star against a sky full of stars. It’s a short promotional clip that invites viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: No transcript available; summary is from the description only.
Central message: Copilot helps Christa 'find the star within' amid a sky of stars.
Promotional, inspirational tone (emoji used in description).
Clear call to action: download and try Copilot via link in bio.
Published by Microsoft Copilot on 2026-01-29.
takeaways: The video contains no technical or operational information relevant to grid or data center decisions.
As promotional content, it’s useful for branding or employee engagement but not for procurement evaluation.
If considering Copilot for operational use, obtain product specs, security/compliance details, and hands-on trials from official Microsoft resources.
Treat this clip as a consumer-facing invitation to try the product, not as documentation for deployment.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This NVIDIA GeForce video highlights the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build contest (u/JeffersonPutnam), featuring a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The description lists full parts including a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5‑6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
bullets: Winner: r/buildapc contest entry by u/JeffersonPutnam.
Form factor: Fractal Design Era 2 small-form-factor chassis.
GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
Key components: MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5‑6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
Full parts list available on PCPartPicker; video hosted by GeForce host Dwight.
takeaways: The build pairs a high-end consumer GPU (GeForce RTX 5090) with a compact SFF chassis, showing high‑power GPUs are being used in space-constrained systems.
Power delivery is notable: the build uses a 1000W SFX PSU (Corsair SF1000), highlighting higher power requirements for similar GPU-equipped systems.
Cooling approach includes a 240mm AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS), indicating substantial thermal management is applied even in small enclosures.
The system is built from consumer desktop components (mini-ITX motherboard, desktop CPU, Founders Edition GPU), so deployment considerations differ from enterprise/datacenter hardware.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy AI solutions, leveraging GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure to streamline operations and boost developer productivity. The collaboration compressed TTEC’s digital transformation timeline and unlocked new revenue streams by expanding what’s possible for the business.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations and increase developer productivity.
The partnership compressed TTEC’s digital transformation timeline.
Efforts unlocked entirely new revenue streams and expanded business ambitions.
Google Cloud Consulting is presented as a resource to help other companies pursue similar transformations (cloud.google.com/consulting).
takeaways: Robust cloud infrastructure is essential to support GenAI deployments and speed time-to-value.
Partnering with cloud consulting teams can compress transformation timelines and enable faster prototyping.
Improving developer productivity and operational efficiency can directly contribute to new revenue opportunities.
Demonstrated success with GenAI suggests prioritizing scalable, managed cloud services for enterprise AI initiatives.
